items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Exploit for CVE-2026-20896 exploit
id: 5335f2f3-aab2-57d1-b41d-42dccc2904a4
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-20896 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-20896
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/08
modified: 2026/07/08
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-20896 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-08T06:46:21Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-20896", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-20896 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:1022607; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-08, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Single source with minimal corroborating evidence
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: CVE-2026-40139: A critical pre-authentication vulnerability exists in the authentication subsystem of BeyondTrust Remote Support.Improp
items.description: A critical pre-authentication vulnerability exists in the authentication subsystem of BeyondTrust Remote Support.Improper processing of authentication requests may allow an unauthenticated remote attacker to bypass access controls and gain unauthorized access to the appliance, including accounts with elevated privileges. Exploitation requires a specific authentication configuration to be enabled.

items.title:
CVE-2026-5268: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists in
the default SFTP server component utilized across the Ciena products li
items.description:
An authentication bypass vulnerability exists in
the default SFTP server component utilized across the Ciena products listed. This vulnerability allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to bypass
security controls and gain unauthorized access to the underlying filesystem.
Successful exploitation could allow an attacker to read or modify system files.

description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-40141 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-40141
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/08
modified: 2026/07/08
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-40141 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-08T06:46:21Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-40141", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-40141 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:4628738; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-08, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: High confidence based on: CVSS score 9.9 (Critical); EPSS score 0.407 (elevated exploitation probability)
items.recommended_sla_action: PRIORITY PATCH — High severity with confirmed threat activity

items.title: CVE-2026-13708: Imager::File::JPEG versions before 1.003 for Perl leak heap memory when reading a JPEG with repeated APP13 markers in i_
items.description:
Imager::File::JPEG versions before 1.003 for Perl leak heap memory when reading a JPEG with repeated APP13 markers in i_readjpeg_wiol.

i_readjpeg_wiol walks the marker list libjpeg returns and, for each APP13 marker, allocates a new buffer with *iptc_itext = mymalloc(...) and overwrites the previous pointer without freeing it. Only the final payload is later turned into a Perl scalar and freed, so a JPEG with N such markers leaks the first N-1 payloads on every read.

In a long-lived process, such as an upload or thumbnailing service, repeated reads accumulate these leaks and exhaust available memory, a denial of service.

The same handler ships bundled in the Imager distribution, where versions before 1.032 are affected and the fix ships in 1.032.
items.apex_ai_summary: [HIGH] CVE advisory. Risk score 7.5/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
items.ioc_note: Actor-attribution campaign — IOC set derived from threat actor research, not automated feed extraction.

apex.ai_summary: [P1] CVE / Vulnerability - high severity (Risk 7.5/10). No MITRE ATT&CK techniques mapped - limited adversarial context. 1 indicator detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. AI confidence: LOW (22%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: CRITICAL PRIORITY - Patch within 24-48 hours. Activate IR playbook. Notify SOC lead and CISO.

ioc_paywall.message: 1 IOC(s) at 7.0% confidence - unlock with Pro tier.

items.title: CVE-2025-53827: ownCloud Core is the server-side component of the file storage, synchronization, and sharing application ownCloud Classi
items.description: ownCloud Core is the server-side component of the file storage, synchronization, and sharing application ownCloud Classic. In versions prior to 10.15.3, the Updater on ownCloud 10 before 10.15.3 has an exposed dangerous method or function. Attackers with administrative privileges may leverage functionality to execute arbitrary code. This issue has been fixed in version 10.15.3.
